possibleWorlds.name: Current state
possibleWorlds.description: Alice is a senior engineer assigned to the payments team

possibleWorlds.name: Role-change scenario
possibleWorlds.description: Alice moves to the docs team, which has no admin requirement

possibleWorlds.name: Contractor scenario
possibleWorlds.description: Alice's employment changes to contractor; contractor policy forbids admin

propositions.justification: Admin access is NOT true in w2 and w3 — necessity fails.
Admin access IS true in w1 (actual world). Possibility holds.

propositions.content: Alice MUST have admin access when on payments team with senior engineer role
propositions.justification: In every world where alice_is_senior_engineer AND alice_on_payments_team, admin is required.

inferences.conclusion: Alice MUST have admin access (conditional necessity under current role)
inferences.justification: Necessitation: if P is provable from the role policy axioms, then □P holds in this frame.

premises: w2 and w3 are accessible from w1
inferences.conclusion: Alice's admin access is NOT unconditionally necessary
inferences.justification: Countermodel: w2 and w3 falsify unconditional necessity. ◇¬(admin) holds.
